# Test Fixtures

Real media files used for integration testing. Each file is a 10-second,
640x360 clip from the Big Buck Bunny short film.

## Files

### Video-only

| File | Codec | Container | Size | Source |
|------|-------|-----------|------|--------|
| `bigbuckbunny_h264.mp4` | H.264 | MP4 | ~1 MB | test-videos.co.uk |
| `bigbuckbunny_h265.mp4` | H.265/HEVC | MP4 | ~1 MB | test-videos.co.uk |
| `bigbuckbunny_vp9.webm` | VP9 | WebM | ~1 MB | test-videos.co.uk |
| `bigbuckbunny_av1.mp4` | AV1 | MP4 | ~480 KB | Transcoded from H.264 fixture |
| `bigbuckbunny_h264.mkv` | H.264 | MKV | ~1 MB | Remuxed from H.264 MP4 fixture |

### Audio + Video

| File | Video | Audio | Container | Size | Source |
|------|-------|-------|-----------|------|--------|
| `bigbuckbunny_h264_aac.mp4` | H.264 | AAC (44.1 kHz, mono) | MP4 | ~1.1 MB | H.264 video + synthetic 440 Hz tone |
| `bigbuckbunny_vp9_opus.webm` | VP9 | Opus (48 kHz, mono) | WebM | ~1.2 MB | VP9 video + synthetic 440 Hz tone |

## Provenance

All video files are sourced from [test-videos.co.uk](https://test-videos.co.uk),
which provides test clips from the Blender Foundation's
[Big Buck Bunny](https://peach.blender.org/) short film.

Big Buck Bunny is released under the
[Creative Commons Attribution 3.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0/)
license by the Blender Foundation.

## Reproduction

The AV1 fixture was generated from the H.264 source:

```bash
ffmpeg -i bigbuckbunny_h264.mp4 -c:v libsvtav1 -crf 40 -preset 8 -an -y bigbuckbunny_av1.mp4
```

The audio+video fixtures were generated by combining existing video-only
fixtures with a synthetic 440 Hz sine tone:

```bash
# H.264 + AAC in MP4
ffmpeg -i bigbuckbunny_h264.mp4 -f lavfi -i "sine=frequency=440:sample_rate=44100:duration=10" \
  -c:v copy -c:a aac -b:a 128k -shortest -y bigbuckbunny_h264_aac.mp4

# VP9 + Opus in WebM
ffmpeg -i bigbuckbunny_vp9.webm -f lavfi -i "sine=frequency=440:sample_rate=48000:duration=10" \
  -c:v copy -c:a libopus -b:a 128k -shortest -y bigbuckbunny_vp9_opus.webm
```

## Notes

- Video-only files are used for video codec integration tests. Audio codec
  integration testing also uses inline synthetic data (encode → decode
  round-trips) in `crates/splica-codec/tests/aac_integration.rs` and
  `crates/splica-codec/tests/opus_integration.rs`.
- Audio+video files are used for end-to-end pipeline tests and benchmark demos.
- These files are small enough (~1 MB each) for regular git storage.
  Git LFS is not required.
